plan_orientation: lower-bound-first: thm:rate-characterization (headline) + thm:minimax-lower are the in-scope sharp converse over the baseline observed-law class and are fully formalizable now (no Defer dependency); oeq:feasible-upper is a CONDITIONAL achievability theorem stated over def:upper-risk with the estimator/fold side-conditions bound as explicit Prop hypotheses. ROUND-3 DISCHARGE (review FLAG-substrate-assumed): the two localized finite-VC empirical-process conditions ass:vc-localized-envelope and ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope are SUBSTRATE FACTS (empirical-process theorems), not modeling assumptions; under the substrate-fact policy they cannot remain plain assumed hypotheses. Causalean PROVES them axiom-clean -- ass:vc-localized-envelope = Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_uniform_deviation_expectation (fixed-radius in-expectation envelope, LocalizedEnvelopeExpectation.lean:67) and ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope = Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_offset_expectation (self-localizing offset positive-part envelope, LocalizedEnvelopeExpectation.lean:258, which already bundles the margin coupling norm(F i)<=A*(Delta i)^kappa and yields the (B^2/n)^{A_alpha}(log n)^p rate) -- so both are DISCHARGED (route 1) per the SUBSTRATE-BUILT directive: encoded as REUSE-DISCHARGE LEMMA nodes (lean_kind:lemma, disposition:reuse + a real reuse decl, defer_tier:false) over those decls, bridged by the in-scope thm:margin-localization (regret radius -> L2 norm radius) and the finite-VC critical-radius rate Causalean.Stat.Concentration.vcLocalizedEnvelope (VCLocalizedRegime.lean:1307), and DROPPED from the hyps of every consumer (oeq:feasible-upper, lem:crude-localized-master-bound, lem:localized-vc-process-bound, lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction, lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control). This STRENGTHENS oeq:feasible-upper (two fewer side conditions); the achievability stays genuinely conditional on the estimator-side nuisance/fold conditions. No Defer node, so the plan is formalizable-now. oeq:feasible-tight (strict-gap tightness) remains the genuinely OPEN research residual, STATED as an open-question Prop def (lean_kind:def, defer_tier:false, excluded from the theorems manifest, no theorem depends_on it) per the OEQ rule -- not a Defer node and not a proof obligation.

env.world: Offline i.i.d. observed-law policy-learning experiment: a probability measure P on the observation space Omega = X x {0,1} x [-1,1], with covariate marginal P_X, propensity e_P, overlap p_P, outcome regressions mu_0/mu_1, contrast tau_P, deterministic binary policies pi, a finite-VC policy class Pi, oracle pi_star, welfare V_P and regret R_P, and disagreement set D_pi.
env.notes: bypass-justified: Causalean PO framework (../Causalean/PO/, API.md §8h: POSystem, POBackdoorSystem.propScore/eLift, consistency/unconfoundedness) is at a CAUSAL-STRUCTURAL abstraction (potential outcomes, regimes, counterfactual operators). The note's honest_scope makes ass:consistency/ass:unconfoundedness OPTIONAL non-load-bearing semantics: the headline and achievability live purely at the observed-law level (P a Mathlib `Measure` on Omega + a policy class). So bind P as `MeasureTheory.Measure` over Mathlib, not POSystem. This S-block binds only the observed-law SYMBOLS (define-local Mathlib measure world); the i.i.d. SAMPLING MODEL (ass:iid) is bound separately by env block S4, which REUSES the cluster primitive Causalean.Stat.IIDSample directly (not a local probability-measure Prop). Search trace: grep ../doc/API.md 'policy|welfare|regret' -> no observed-law policy-learning/welfare-regret world exists; PO/ATE modules are causal-structural -> define-local observed-law world over Mathlib.

env.world: Real-valued exponent/constant parameter block: margin/overlap exponents and the derived information exponents, scales, and schedule constants.
env.notes: Plain `ℝ`/`ℝ≥0`/`ℕ` parameters and `ℝ`-valued derived exponents (beta_ag, D_ag, r_star via def:exponents) and schedules (h_n, q_n, etc.). No substrate world needed; these are scalars bound at statement-level by `variable`/def. Search trace: scalar parameters, no reuse target sought.

env.world: Estimator/construction block: the clipped AIPW score functional, the feasible cross-fit ERM, the minimax and feasible-upper risk functionals, and the two-point witness active block.
env.notes: These symbols ARE the core's own constructions (def:clipped-aipw-score, def:feasible-erm, def:minimax-regret, def:upper-risk, def:two-point-witness); bound by those def nodes. AIPW score is structurally close to Causalean.Estimation.ATE AIPW influence functions (API.md §13) but at a different (clipped/policy-indexed, observed-law) abstraction -> bypass-justified define-local; reuse limited to algebraic Mathlib facts.

env.world: I.i.d. sampling model: the n observations O_1,...,O_n are an i.i.d. sample drawn from the observed law P, realized by the exact cluster primitive Causalean.Stat.IIDSample (sequence of measurable maps on a common ambient probability space with mutual independence, identical distribution, and law-match to P).
env.notes: REUSE the cluster i.i.d.-sampling primitive Causalean.Stat.IIDSample (../Causalean/Stat/Sample.lean:42) for the sampling design; its fields Z/meas/indep(iIndepFun)/identDist(IdentDistrib)/law(μ.map(Z 0)=P) carry the full i.i.d. content. The data symbols O, P, n it ranges over are already bound by S1 (no duplicate symbol binding here); this block only fixes the SAMPLING-MODEL world to the reused primitive. The n-fold product experiment formed from an IIDSample term feeds the Le Cam tensorization Causalean.Stat.chiSqDiv_prod (thm:minimax-lower) and the foldwise i.i.d. restriction used by the cross-fit reductions. This replaces the round-1 define-local local Prop (which only asserted the one-observation law was a probability measure and missed the i.i.d. sampling content -- round-2 fix FLAG-unjustified-rebuild). Verified present: structure IIDSample at ../Causalean/Stat/Sample.lean:42.

ass:iid.notes: REUSE the exact cluster i.i.d.-sampling primitive `Causalean.Stat.IIDSample (Ω X : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] [MeasurableSpace X] (μ : Measure Ω) (P : Measure X)` (../Causalean/Stat/Sample.lean:42), whose fields Z/meas/indep/identDist/law encode the FULL i.i.d. sampling content: measurable sample points `Z i`, mutual independence `iIndepFun Z μ`, identical distribution `IdentDistrib (Z 0) (Z i)`, and law-match `μ.map (Z 0) = P`. ass:iid is realized by carrying a term `(S : IIDSample Ω O μ P)` as the sampling hypothesis -- NOT a local Prop `IsIIDSample P := IsProbabilityMeasure P.dataMeasure`, which asserts only that the one-observation law is a probability measure and omits the i.i.d. sampling content entirely (round-2 fix FLAG-unjustified-rebuild). Consumed inline by thm:minimax-lower (the n-fold product experiment formed from S, paired with Causalean.Stat.chiSqDiv_prod tensorization) and by the cross-fit reductions lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction / lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control (each balanced evaluation fold is i.i.d. via the same IIDSample bundle restricted to that fold). Verified present: `structure IIDSample` at ../Causalean/Stat/Sample.lean:42 with the five stated fields; the sampling world is bound by env block S4.

ass:bounded-outcome.notes: Prop: Y(a) in [-1,1]; member of def:law-class and inline hyp of thm:welfare-identity etc. Routine bound, define-local.

ass:positivity.notes: Prop: 0<e_P<1 P_X-a.s.; member of def:law-class, inline hyp of lem:clip-bias. Causalean.POBackdoorSystem overlap predicate is the causal analogue (API.md §9g); bypass-justified observed-law Prop.

ass:margin.notes: Prop: Tsybakov margin P(0<|tau|<=u)<=C_m u^alpha. Member of def:law-class. Search trace: grep ../doc/API.md 'margin' -> no Tsybakov-margin primitive in Stat/Estimation -> define-local.

ass:zero-effect.notes: Prop: canonical zero-effect region; member of def:law-class. define-local.

ass:overlap-decay.notes: NOVEL class-defining Prop: P{p_P<=v,0<|tau|<=u}<=C_o u^alpha v^(1/gamma). Member of def:law-class; the paper's contribution lives here. Search trace: grep ../doc/API.md 'overlap' -> SCM/ID/Overlap, WeakOverlap inverse-moment (§12wo) all at different abstraction (no coupled margin/overlap tail) -> define-local.

ass:policy-class.notes: Prop: pointwise-measurable finite-VC class with countable pointwise-dense Pi_0. VC-dimension machinery (vcDim, Sauer-Shelah) exists in Causalean.Stat.Concentration (API.md §12'.e, ../Causalean/Stat/Concentration/VCLocalizedRegime.lean) and can supply the d_Pi field; the pointwise-dense skeleton is define-local. Search trace: API.md §12' vcDim/BinaryFactoredVCClass -> reuse VC notion in field, Prop stated local.

ass:optimal-in-class.notes: Prop: pi_star in Pi. inline hyp of lem:feasible-erm-basic-inequality and the achievability track. define-local.

ass:margin-window.notes: Prop: 0<u_0<2 normalization; inline hyp of lem:witness-membership and thm:minimax-lower (gives existence of off-block contrast tau_0 in (u_0,2)). define-local trivial bound.

ass:nuisance-rate.notes: Prop: cross-fit L2 rates r_mu_n, r_e_n with product O(n^-1/2). DML rate condition (Chernozhukov 2018); analogue to Causalean.Estimation.OrthogonalMoments / ATE nuisance-rate hypotheses (API.md §12g/§13). Bound as explicit conditional hyp on real nuisance estimators (F2.5). define-local Prop over r_mu_n,r_e_n schedule.

ass:strict-overlap-endpoint.notes: Prop: gamma=0 => p_P>=underline_p a.s.; member of def:law-class. define-local.

ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances.notes: Approved added Prop: muhat_a in [-1,1]; estimator-side conditional hyp for the achievability track. define-local trivial truncation.

ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents.notes: Approved added Prop: r_mu_n<=C_mu n^-a, r_mu_n r_e_n<=C_prod n^-c. Polynomial rate schedule feeding clip-balance. define-local Prop over (a,c,C_mu,C_prod).

ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count.notes: Approved added Prop: K fixed finite, balanced deterministic fold partition. define-local; consumed by the cross-fit reduction lemmas.

def:welfare-regret.notes: Construction: V_P(pi)=E_P[pi(X) tau_P(X)], pi_star=1{tau_P>=0}, R_P(pi)=V_P(pi_star)-V_P(pi). define-local over Mathlib integral; binds V_P, R_P, pi_star (S1). Search trace: grep ../doc/API.md 'welfare|regret' -> none -> define-local.

def:exponents.notes: Construction (inputs alpha,gamma): beta_ag=(0 if gamma=0 else alpha*gamma/(alpha+1)); D_ag=2+alpha+beta_ag; r_star=(1+alpha)/D_ag. define-local real arithmetic; binds beta_ag,D_ag,r_star (S2).

def:feasible-rate.notes: Construction (inputs alpha,gamma): compact-set maximizer of phi giving (s_feas,t_feas,g_joint) and r_feas=min{r_star,g_joint}; gamma=0 branch r_feas=min{A_alpha,c}. define-local; binds s_feas,r_feas (S2). Existence of maximizer = continuity on compact set (Mathlib IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn).

def:clipped-aipw-score.notes: Construction: Gamma_q(O;eta)=mu_1-mu_0+(A/e_q)(Y-mu_1)-((1-A)/(1-e_q))(Y-mu_0). Structural analogue of Causalean.Estimation.ATE AIPW influence (API.md §13) but clipped & policy-indexed at observed-law level -> bypass-justified define-local; binds Gamma_q (S3). Search trace: grep ../doc/API.md 'AIPW' -> ATE/ATT/DTR AIPW exist but uncapped, different abstraction -> define-local.

def:feasible-erm.notes: Construction: measurable 1/n-near-maximizer over countable pointwise-dense Pi_0 with deterministic balanced K-fold cross-fit. define-local; binds pi_hat_n (S3). Measurability via countable skeleton (proved in lem:feasible-erm-basic-inequality).

def:minimax-regret.notes: Construction: M_n=inf over estimators sup over P in P_class of E_P R_P. define-local iInf/iSup; binds M_n (S3). Shape parallels Causalean.Stat.Minimax minimax-risk patterns (API.md §12w) but over the bespoke regret loss -> define-local.

def:upper-risk.notes: Construction: regime-indexed conditional U_n = sup E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) over P in def:law-class WITH ass:optimal-in-class, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, ass:policy-class, ass:vc-localized-envelope, ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope, ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count. F2.5: the supremum domain bundles the real estimator/fold/process side-conditions; binds U_n (S3). define-local.

def:law-class.notes: CLASS structure P_class(alpha,gamma): one field per member-property, each typed by the corresponding ass:* Prop def. Members == core by_member_properties exactly. The baseline observed-law class for the headline lower bound. define-local.

def:two-point-witness.notes: Construction (inputs n,alpha,gamma): explicit pair (P_{n,+},P_{n,-}) on X=[0,1] with Lebesgue P_X, active block B_n, h_n/q_n scales, charged treated cell. CRUX-supporting explicit least-favorable family. define-local over Mathlib measures (Lebesgue, Bernoulli on {-1,1}); binds B_n (S3). Search trace: grep ../doc/API.md 'two.point|least.favorable|witness' -> Minimax provides the TESTING engine not the construction -> define-local.

thm:welfare-identity.notes: Build-inline: pointwise sign analysis + integral. Foundational shared identity. proof_tex complete.

thm:margin-localization.notes: Build-inline: Tsybakov margin -> P_X(D_pi)<=C R_P^{alpha/(1+alpha)}, u-optimization. Shared by the achievability empirical-process lemmas. proof_tex complete.

prop:overlap-envelope.notes: Build-inline: algebra h^a>=h^b iff a<=b pinning beta_ag as max admissible weak-arm exponent. proof_tex complete.

lem:witness-membership.notes: Build-inline (crux support): verifies each def:law-class member-property for the explicit twoPointWitness via prop:overlap-envelope tight window. Hyps are the law-side member assumptions (each in depends_on, directly listed). proof_tex complete.

lem:two-point-divergence.notes: Build-inline (crux support): per-observation chi^2 <= C h_n^{D_ag} on the charged cell, tensorize via Causalean.Stat.chiSqDiv_prod (../Causalean/Stat/Minimax/ChiSquared.lean:177) to a bounded n-fold divergence. REUSE: chiSqDiv, chiSqDiv_prod. proof_tex complete.

lem:regret-separation.notes: Build-inline (crux support): opposite optimal labels on B_n force regret >= c h_n^{1+alpha} via thm:welfare-identity. proof_tex complete.

lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq.notes: RE-PLANNED define-local (round-3 FLAG-reuse-shape): the prior reuse target Causalean.Stat.two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le (MinimaxRisk.lean:87) is a REAL-VALUED ESTIMATOR miss-probability bound (params est,θ₀,θ₁,s; conclusion on max miss probability), NOT this core node, which is a BINARY-TEST lemma: chi^2(P_+^{⊗n}||P_-^{⊗n}) <= C_chi  =>  every test psi has P_+^n(psi≠+)+P_-^n(psi≠-) >= c(C_chi)>0, plus the per-observation <= c_0/n corollary via the product identity. Build-inline as a thin testing lemma from the CLOSER substrate facts the core proof_tex actually uses: (1) Causalean.Stat.one_sub_tvDist_le_test (../Causalean/Stat/Minimax/TotalVariation.lean:100): 1-tvDist μ ν <= μ.real A + ν.real Aᶜ gives the testing-error floor 1-TV; (2) Causalean.Stat.tvDist_le_half_sqrt_chiSqDiv (../Causalean/Stat/Minimax/ChiSquared.lean:58): TV <= ½√χ² converts the chi-square bound to a TV bound (the core's Cauchy-Schwarz/Pinsker-type step; the slightly sharper t^2<=C_chi(1-t) form in proof_tex is an in-scope elementary refinement over the same TV<=½√χ² substrate); (3) Causalean.Stat.chiSqDiv_prod (../Causalean/Stat/Minimax/ChiSquared.lean:177): product/tensorization identity giving 1+χ²(P_+^{⊗n}||P_-^{⊗n})=(1+χ²(P_+||P_-))^n <= e^{c_0} for the per-observation corollary. Causalean.Stat.Minimax.LeCam (LeCam.lean:70) is the ANALOGOUS module carrying exactly this TV-testing + triangle pattern; F3 adapts its one_sub_tvDist_le_test usage to the two-point (no triangle) case. REUSE limited to these factored substrate lemmas; the summed-test-error packaging is the node's own ~1-file routine content. Search trace: grep ../Causalean/Stat/Minimax 'one_sub_tvDist_le_test|tvDist_le_half_sqrt_chiSqDiv|chiSqDiv_prod' -> all three present (TotalVariation.lean:100, ChiSquared.lean:58/177); two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le is estimator-shaped, not test-shaped -> define-local thin wrapper over the TV/chi-square substrate.

thm:minimax-lower.notes: Build-inline CRUX (the novel sharp converse): Le Cam two-point reduction assembling lem:witness-membership + lem:two-point-divergence + lem:regret-separation through the LOCAL binary-test lemma lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq (now define-local: chi^2 product control => constant testing-error floor) to M_n >= c h_n^{1+alpha} = c n^{-r_star}. Per round-3 re-plan, the testing step routes through the local le_cam_two_point_chisq (a thin wrapper over Causalean.Stat one_sub_tvDist_le_test + tvDist_le_half_sqrt_chiSqDiv + chiSqDiv_prod), NOT the estimator-shaped Causalean.Stat.two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le. NOT assumed -- the least-favorable family is CONSTRUCTED; the construction & coupling are the theorem's own content. The ass:iid hypothesis is carried as a reused Causalean.Stat.IIDSample bundle (env S4), from which the n-fold product experiment is formed for the chiSqDiv_prod tensorization. proof_tex complete.

oeq:feasible-upper.notes: Build-inline CONDITIONAL achievability theorem (status proved). F2.5: stated over def:upper-risk with the estimator/fold conditions bound as REAL Prop hypotheses (def:law-class covers the law-side member assumptions bounded-outcome/margin/overlap-decay/strict-overlap-endpoint; the estimator-side assumptions are listed directly). Assembles lem:crude-localized-master-bound + lem:clip-balance-exponent + def:feasible-rate to U_n <= C n^{-r_feas}(log n)^p. ROUND-3 DISCHARGE: the two localized finite-VC empirical-process conditions ass:vc-localized-envelope / ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope are NO LONGER hypotheses -- they are substrate FACTS proved in Causalean (localized_uniform_deviation_expectation / localized_offset_expectation), now discharged to reuse lemmas and USED via lem:localized-vc-process-bound / lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control, not assumed. This STRENGTHENS the theorem (two fewer side conditions); the achievability remains genuinely conditional on the estimator-side nuisance/fold conditions (honest_scope). All sub-lemmas are build-inline (Helpers.lean). Not an unconditional minimax upper claim. proof_tex complete.

thm:rate-characterization.notes: Build-inline HEADLINE (lower-bound only): one-line corollary M_n >= c n^{-r_star}, r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_ag), of thm:minimax-lower + def:exponents. No feasible-upper / tightness dependency per design (d0_rate_cycle_repair). proof_tex complete.

oeq:feasible-tight.notes: OPEN OEQ (core kind:openendedquestion, status:to-prove) -- STATED as a named Prop def, NOT a theorem (F2.5 fix-locus: prior plan miscoded it as a theorem, forcing a permanent sorry). Per the OEQ rule it is a Prop def:FeasibleTightQuestion stating the strict-gap tightness residual: whether any genuinely feasible estimator under estimated cross-fitted nuisances attains the converse exponent r_star in the branch g_joint<r_star, or weak-arm nuisance learning imposes the slower conditional g_joint of oeq:feasible-upper. It is NOT a proof obligation and NOT buildable infrastructure: defer_tier:false, EXCLUDED from the theorems manifest, and no theorem depends_on it. F2 scaffolds only the Prop statement comparing thm:minimax-lower's converse benchmark with oeq:feasible-upper's conditional exponent over def:feasible-rate -- no proof, no sorry-bearing theorem. The note's acknowledged open problem (d0_open_oeq_residuals, honest_scope, f1_defer_is_open_problem): open mathematics, not missing substrate. Search trace: open research question, no substrate resolves it; stated, not proven.

lem:clip-bias.notes: Build-inline: GENUINE conditional-expectation drift identity (NOT free-standing algebra). SCAFFOLD-REDIRECT fix: the scaffold previously stated clip_bias as a pure-algebra identity over the nuisance fields that never touched dataMeasure, so it was not the NL conditional-expectation drift identity. Fixed in the sym:O cluster: WellFormedLaw (Basic.lean) now carries the observed-law SEMANTIC conditions tying dataMeasure to e_P, mu_0, mu_1 (tested conditional-expectation form e_P=P(A=1|X), mu_a=E[Y|A=a,X]); clip_bias is restated against P.dataMeasure as the tested identity ∫ φ(X) Γ_q dP - ∫ φ τ dP_X = ∫ φ b_q dP_X (i.e. E_P[Γ_q|X]=τ+b_q a.s.), with WellFormedLaw P (semantic tie) and BoundedOutcome P (regularity, threaded from def:law-class) added as premises plus a bounded measurable covariate test function φ. The WellFormedLaw strengthening propagates to every def:law-class consumer (oeq:feasible-upper, oeq:feasible-tight, lem:crude-localized-master-bound) via LawClass.wf without further textual edits. proof_tex complete (the tested identity is the genuinely-new sorry obligation).

lem:feasible-erm-basic-inequality.notes: Build-inline: measurability of feasibleERM via countable skeleton + 1/n basic inequality. proof_tex complete.

lem:localized-vc-self-bound.notes: Build-inline: deterministic peeling from the offset bound proved in lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control (dependency direction offset-control -> self-bound). proof_tex complete.

lem:crude-localized-master-bound.notes: Build-inline: the pooled crude q^{-2}-envelope master bound assembling basic inequality + clip-bias drift + offset-control + self-bound. The achievability backbone. Law-side member assumptions (bounded-outcome, strict-overlap-endpoint) covered by def:law-class. ROUND-3 DISCHARGE: the localized finite-VC empirical-process conditions ass:vc-localized-envelope / ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope are NO LONGER hypotheses -- they are substrate facts discharged to reuse lemmas and USED via lem:localized-vc-process-bound / lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control (which now carry the Causalean envelope theorems internally), not assumed. proof_tex complete.

lem:localized-vc-process-bound.notes: Build-inline: fixed-radius localized process bound combining thm:margin-localization (regret->disagreement mass) with the DISCHARGED reuse lemma vc_localized_envelope (= Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_uniform_deviation_expectation). ROUND-3 DISCHARGE: ass:vc-localized-envelope is NO LONGER a hypothesis -- it is a substrate fact discharged to a reuse lemma and USED here (the Causalean fixed-radius envelope applied after the margin-localization bridge), not assumed. proof_tex complete.

lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction.notes: Build-inline: foldwise application of lem:localized-vc-process-bound + balanced fixed-K collapse of weights. ROUND-3 DISCHARGE: ass:vc-localized-envelope is NO LONGER a hypothesis -- it is a substrate fact discharged to a reuse lemma and USED (internally, via lem:localized-vc-process-bound -> Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_uniform_deviation_expectation), not assumed. The ass:iid hyp is the reused Causalean.Stat.IIDSample bundle (env S4); each balanced evaluation fold inherits its i.i.d. structure by restricting that bundle to the fold. proof_tex complete.

lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control.notes: Build-inline: pooled offset positive-part control by conditioning on training folds and applying the DISCHARGED reuse lemma vc_localized_offset_envelope (= Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_offset_expectation) foldwise. ROUND-3 DISCHARGE: ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope is NO LONGER a hypothesis -- it is a substrate fact discharged to a reuse lemma and USED here (the Causalean self-localizing offset envelope applied to each evaluation fold via the margin-coupling bridge thm:margin-localization), not assumed. Stochastic input to lem:localized-vc-self-bound; must NOT depend on it (dependency direction fixed by d0_offset_cycle_repair). The ass:iid hyp is the reused Causalean.Stat.IIDSample bundle (env S4); conditioning on the training folds leaves each evaluation fold i.i.d. via that bundle. proof_tex complete.

lem:localized-clipped-drift-bound.notes: Build-inline: deterministic drift bound from lem:clip-bias + lem:clipped-region-localization + Cauchy-Schwarz. proof_tex complete.

lem:crude-clipped-score-envelope.notes: Build-inline: |Gamma_q|<=C/q, E[Gamma_q^2|X]<=C/q^2 from clipped denominators + bounded outcomes/nuisances. proof_tex complete (the crude q^{-2} scale).

lem:clipped-region-localization.notes: Build-inline: P_X(D_pi ∩ {p_P<=q}) <= C u^alpha q^{1/gamma}+r/u via ass:overlap-decay + thm:welfare-identity. proof_tex complete.

lem:clip-balance-exponent.notes: Build-inline: optimize the five master-bound terms over the def:feasible-rate schedule to r_up=r_feas. Real-exponent arithmetic on a compact set. proof_tex complete.

message: Lower-bound-first plan: headline converse (thm:rate-characterization/thm:minimax-lower) is fully build-inline reusing Causalean Le Cam chi^2 two-point substrate; ass:iid REUSES the exact cluster primitive Causalean.Stat.IIDSample (env S4). Round-3 fix (review FLAG-substrate-assumed): the two localized finite-VC empirical-process conditions ass:vc-localized-envelope (fixed-radius) and ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope (offset positive-part) are SUBSTRATE FACTS proven axiom-clean in Causalean, so they are DISCHARGED (route 1) per the SUBSTRATE-BUILT directive -- encoded as REUSE-DISCHARGE LEMMA nodes (lean_kind:lemma, disposition:reuse + real reuse decl, defer_tier:false) over Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_uniform_deviation_expectation and Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_offset_expectation respectively (bridged by the in-scope thm:margin-localization + finite-VC critical-radius rate vcLocalizedEnvelope) and DROPPED from the hyps of every consumer (oeq:feasible-upper, lem:crude-localized-master-bound, lem:localized-vc-process-bound, lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction, lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control). This STRENGTHENS oeq:feasible-upper (two fewer side conditions); it remains an honest CONDITIONAL achievability theorem over the estimator-side nuisance/fold conditions. ass:iid/S4 reuse unchanged (reviewer-confirmed type-fit). No Defer item remains. oeq:feasible-tight remains an OPEN-question Prop def. Verdict: formalizable-now (no Defer nodes).
feasibility_notes: Confirmed reusable (grep-verified in ../Causalean): Causalean.Stat.IIDSample (Stat/Sample.lean:42) -- the exact cluster i.i.d.-sampling structure (fields Z/meas/indep:iIndepFun/identDist:IdentDistrib/law:μ.map(Z 0)=P) -- is REUSED directly for ass:iid and the S4 sampling-model world; the n-fold product experiment / foldwise i.i.d. restriction feed thm:minimax-lower and the cross-fit reductions. Causalean.Stat.one_sub_tvDist_le_test (Stat/Minimax/TotalVariation.lean:100), tvDist_le_half_sqrt_chiSqDiv (Stat/Minimax/ChiSquared.lean:58), chiSqDiv / chiSqDiv_prod (Stat/Minimax/ChiSquared.lean:177) give the factored testing-floor + TV-to-chi-square + tensorization substrate; lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq is a BUILD-INLINE thin binary-test lemma over these (Causalean.Stat.Minimax.LeCam analogous). The PO framework and ATE/AIPW modules are at a causal-structural / uncapped-influence abstraction; honest_scope makes consistency/unconfoundedness non-load-bearing, so the observed-law world (P a Mathlib Measure on X x {0,1} x [-1,1] + finite-VC policy class) is define-local over Mathlib (bypass-justified). Build-inline (in scope): all definitions/structures, both shared theorems (welfare-identity, margin-localization), prop:overlap-envelope, the three lower-bound construction lemmas + thm:minimax-lower (CRUX -- explicit least-favorable family constructed, testing engine reused) and thm:rate-characterization (headline corollary). LOCALIZED EMPIRICAL-PROCESS SUBSTRATE (this round's fix -- review FLAG-substrate-assumed): the two localized finite-VC empirical-process conditions ass:vc-localized-envelope (fixed-radius) and ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope (offset positive-part) are SUBSTRATE FACTS (general empirical-process theorems), not modeling assumptions, so under the substrate-fact policy they may not remain plain assumed hypotheses. Confirmed reusable by reading the decl signatures: Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_uniform_deviation_expectation (LocalizedEnvelopeExpectation.lean:67-86) gives the in-expectation fixed-radius localized envelope E[uniformDeviation of the radius-r localization] <= 2*r*criticalRadius(R.psi n) for a LocalizedRegime over a Countable index, and Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_offset_expectation (LocalizedEnvelopeExpectation.lean:258-302) gives the self-localizing offset positive-part envelope E[sup_i max 0 (2|(P_n-P)F i|-Delta i/4)] <= offsetPeelingConstantC(1/8,kappa)*(16 rho A)^{1/(1-kappa)}+10 rho^2+4 R.b delta, ALREADY bundling the caller margin coupling norm(F i)<=A*(Delta i)^kappa with Delta:=regret and the (B^2/n)^{A_alpha}(log n)^p rate; both are axiom-clean (proven, no sorry/axiom). Therefore both are DISCHARGED (route 1 of the substrate-fact policy) per the SUBSTRATE-BUILT directive: encoded as REUSE-DISCHARGE LEMMA nodes (lean_kind:lemma, disposition:reuse + a real reuse decl, defer_tier:false), discharged against those Causalean theorems plus the in-scope bridges thm:margin-localization (regret radius -> L2 norm radius) and the finite-VC critical-radius rate Causalean.Stat.Concentration.vcLocalizedEnvelope (VCLocalizedRegime.lean:1307; the countable finite-VC class ass:policy-class supplies the [Countable ι] index and LocalizedRegime). The offset decl localized_offset_expectation takes the margin coupling as a caller-supplied hypothesis hcoupling (signature line 296) furnished by the in-scope thm:margin-localization, so the bridge is built and the directive's defer_tier substrate-gate fallback does not apply. Each is DROPPED from the hyps of every consumer (oeq:feasible-upper, lem:crude-localized-master-bound, lem:localized-vc-process-bound, lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction, lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control), STRENGTHENING oeq:feasible-upper by two fewer side conditions while leaving it genuinely conditional on the estimator-side nuisance/fold conditions. OPEN OEQ (0 Defer nodes): oeq:feasible-tight (strict-gap tightness) is genuinely open mathematics, STATED as a Prop def (lean_kind:def, defer_tier:false, excluded from the theorems manifest, no theorem depends_on it). Verdict: formalizable-now -- no remaining Defer items.
